n.description: Number of vertices in the perfect graph

m.description: Number of edges in the perfect graph

E.description: Edges of the graph; each row lists the two endpoint vertices of an edge, with the smaller vertex index listed first

P.description: Number of disjoint clusters partitioning the vertex set

C.description: Binary cluster membership matrix: C_{i,p} is 1 if vertex i belongs to cluster p, 0 otherwise

assumptions.description: The number of vertices is positive.
The number of edges is non-negative.
Each cluster contains at least one vertex.

code.python: assert all(sum(C[i][p] for i in range(n)) >= 1 for p in range(P))

assumptions.description: The clusters partition the vertex set: every vertex belongs to exactly one cluster.

code.python: assert all(sum(C[i][p] for p in range(P)) == 1 for i in range(n))

assumptions.description: Every edge connects two distinct, valid vertex indices.

code.python: assert all(0 <= i < j <= n - 1 for i, j in E)

assumptions.description: The graph is perfect: every induced subgraph has chromatic number equal to its clique number.

code.python:
G = nx.Graph()
G.add_nodes_from(range(n))
G.add_edges_from(E)
assert nx.is_perfect_graph(G)

y.description: y_k equals 1 if color k is used, 0 otherwise

w.description: w_{i,k} equals 1 if vertex i is selected and assigned color k, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: A vertex can only be assigned color k if color k is used.
No two vertices sharing an edge may receive the same color.
Exactly one vertex is selected and colored from each cluster.

code.gurobipy:
for p in range(P):
    model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(C[i][p] * w[i, k] for i in range(n) for k in range(P)) == 1)

constraints.description: Clique cutting plane: for every clique of the graph, the number of colors used must be at least the number of selected vertices within that clique, since vertices in a clique are pairwise adjacent and therefore must all receive distinct colors.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{k=0}^{P-1} y_k \geq \sum_{i \in S} \sum_{k=0}^{P-1} w_{i,k} \quad \forall \text{ maximal clique } S \subseteq \{0,\ldots,n-1\}

code.gurobipy:
G_temp = nx.Graph()
G_temp.add_nodes_from(range(n))
G_temp.add_edges_from(E)
for clique in nx.find_cliques(G_temp):
    model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(y[k] for k in range(P)) >= gp.quicksum(w[i, k] for i in clique for k in range(P)))

objective.description: Minimize the total number of distinct colors used across all selected vertices.

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gp.quicksum(y[k] for k in range(P)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

notes: This is formulation a (Model 1 of Seker, Ekim, and Taskin (2021)) strengthened with a static clique cutting-plane constraint.

The added constraint lower-bounds the number of colors used by the number of selected vertices within any clique, since pairwise-adjacent selected vertices must receive distinct colors; this is the standard clique-based valid inequality for graph coloring, tightening the LP relaxation of formulation a without changing its feasible integer solutions.

As in formulation b, cliques are not passed in as a parameter: the full set of maximal cliques of the graph is enumerated at solve time using networkx, rather than precomputed or generated iteratively via a cutting-plane algorithm as in the source paper.
As in formulation a, the paper's symmetry-breaking constraint y_k <= y_{k-1} is excluded: it is a solver-acceleration device that is not part of the underlying selective graph coloring problem.
